texto: Power loves not the light of day, nor the attention of curious eyes. In darkness it thrives most...A lord may send his army hither and thither, but the true testing of his power is in those places where his army is not...Has he sent its long fingers far enough through the backstreets and alleys, into the drinking dens and the lending-houses, so that he may gather them unto himself and hold them firm without a single swordsman?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Brian Ruckley que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “Power loves not the light of day, nor the attention of curious eyes. In darkness it thrives most.…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, kings, power, com foco em termino e reflexao-noturna.

frase_original: Power loves not the light of day, nor the attention of curious eyes. In darkness it thrives most...A lord may send his army hither and thither, but the true testing of his power is in those places where his army is not...Has he sent its long fingers far enough through the backstreets and alleys, into the drinking dens and the lending-houses, so that he may gather them unto himself and hold them firm without a single swordsman?

texto: When a man may whisper in a close ear, and that whisper be repeated far away and many moons later, then he has power. When a many may speak against another, and that other be brought to ruin and rue by nothing more than those words, then he has power. And if a man can act without the appearance of action, and bring about great change without the appearance of desiring it, then he has power.
explicacao: Citação de Brian Ruckley sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “When a man may whisper in a close ear, and that whisper be repeated far away and many moons later…” articula reflexao, power, whisper em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e reflexao.
